name: events
context: Slack Events API: subscribe to workspace events, verify requests, handle event payloads
info: The Slack Events API delivers real-time notifications when things happen in a workspace — messages posted, reactions added, channels created, users joining, and more. Events are delivered either via HTTP POST to a Request URL or through Socket Mode WebSocket connections. HTTP delivery requires URL verification (a challenge handshake) and request signature verification using the signing secret. Socket Mode (see [[slack/bots#socket-mode]]) avoids these requirements by connecting outbound. Event payloads include the event type, user, channel, timestamp, and type-specific data. Bolt.js (see [[slack/bots]]) handles all verification and parsing automatically.

sections.title: Event subscription setup
sections.body: Enable Event Subscriptions in app settings. For HTTP mode, provide an HTTPS Request URL that responds to the url_verification challenge. For Socket Mode, generate an app-level token and connect via WebSocket (see [[slack/bots#socket-mode]]). Subscribe to bot events and reinstall the app to apply new scopes. See [[slack#scopes-permissions]] for required scopes per event type.

sections.title: Event types reference
sections.body: Key event types: `message` (channel messages), `app_mention` (bot @mentions), `reaction_added`/`reaction_removed` (emoji reactions), `member_joined_channel`/`member_left_channel` (membership), `channel_created`/`channel_archive` (channel lifecycle), `team_join` (new users), `file_shared` (uploads), `app_home_opened` (App Home tab). Each requires specific OAuth scopes.

sections.title: Request verification
sections.body: Verify HTTP event deliveries using the signing secret: compute HMAC-SHA256 of `v0:timestamp:body` and compare with the `x-slack-signature` header. Reject requests older than 5 minutes. [[slack/bots]] using Bolt.js handle this automatically. URL verification challenge must be answered when first configuring the Request URL.

sections.title: Socket Mode delivery
sections.body: Socket Mode delivers events over WebSocket — no public URL, TLS, or manual verification needed. Enable in app settings, generate an `xapp-` token with `connections:write` scope. Use `@slack/socket-mode` directly or Bolt.js with `socketMode: true` (see [[slack/bots#socket-mode]]). Supports automatic reconnection.

sections.title: Retry handling
sections.body: Slack retries event delivery up to 3 times if no HTTP 200 response within 3 seconds. Retries use exponential backoff. Headers: `X-Slack-Retry-Num`, `X-Slack-Retry-Reason`. Respond with 200 immediately and process asynchronously. Deduplicate using `event_id`. Optionally respond with `X-Slack-No-Retry: 1` to suppress retries.
